nav.signOutConfirmDescription: You'll need to sign in again with your password to access your encrypted files.

auth.argon2idNote: Running Argon2id key derivation (this takes a moment)

totp.title: Two-factor authentication
totp.enterCode: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.

serverSelect.title: Connect to your kutup server
serverSelect.subtitle: Enter the address of your kutup server to continue.
serverSelect.errorInvalid: That doesn't look like a valid URL.
serverSelect.errorInsecureHttp: http:// is only allowed for localhost addresses. Use https:// otherwise.
serverSelect.errorUnreachable: Could not reach a kutup server at this address.
serverSelect.errorNotKutup: This address is reachable but it doesn't appear to be a kutup server.

auth.vaultUnavailable: Stay-signed-in is unavailable on this machine. Install gnome-keyring or libsecret to enable it.

register.title: Create account
register.passwordTooWeak: Password is too weak — choose a stronger one

mnemonic.title: Save your recovery phrase
mnemonic.warning: This 24-word phrase is shown <1>once</1>. Write it down and store it safely. It is the only way to recover your account if you forget your password.

confirm.title: Confirm recovery phrase
confirm.instruction: Type all 24 words to confirm you've saved them.
confirm.placeholder: Enter all 24 words separated by spaces…
confirm.mismatch: Recovery phrase does not match. Check each word carefully.

disabled.title: Registration disabled
disabled.desc: Public registration is currently disabled. Contact your administrator to get access.

recovery.title: Recover account
recovery.description: Enter your 24-word recovery phrase and a new password. 2FA is bypassed during recovery — the recovery phrase is your second factor.

success.title: Account recovered!
success.desc: Your password has been reset. Sign in with your new password.

drive.noSharedFolders: No folders have been shared with you yet.

toast.someFailed: Some items could not be deleted
toast.zipSelectDir: Select a folder to save the parts
toast.zipNoFsa: This folder is too large to download as a ZIP in this browser. Use Chrome or Edge, the desktop app, or the CLI.
toast.zipNoFiles: This folder has no files to download.

deleteFolder.title: Delete folder “{{name}}”?
deleteFolder.desc: All files and subfolders will be permanently deleted.

batchDelete.willBeDeleted: will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.

publicLink.title: Public link ready
publicLink.desc: Anyone with this link can access the files. The decryption key is in the fragment and is never sent to the server.

inviteLink.title: Invite link ready
inviteLink.desc: Send this link to the recipient. They’ll paste it in ‘Add remote share’.

share.title: Share “{{name}}”
share.federatedNote: Federated share — will generate an invite link

addRemote.title: Add remote share
addRemote.description: Paste the invite link you received from someone on another Kutup server.

account.title: Account
account.presenceColorDesc: Your color in collaborative cursors and cell-edit highlights. Stable across notes and office documents.

totp.title: Two-Factor Authentication
totp.active: TOTP is active on this account
totp.disableDesc: This reduces your account security. You will no longer be asked for a code when signing in.
totp.addSecurity: Add an extra layer of security with an authenticator app.
totp.scanQr: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.)
totp.scanInstructions: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app, then enter the 6-digit code it generates.

encryption.title: Encryption
encryption.desc1: All files are encrypted client-side using XChaCha20-Poly1305. Your master key and private key are derived from your password using Argon2id and are never sent to the server. The server stores only ciphertext it cannot decrypt.
encryption.desc2: To change your password, use the

devices.title: Devices
devices.desc: Browser tabs and CLI sessions register here when you start a collaborative edit. Revoke a device to disconnect it and reject future signed updates from it.
devices.revokeDesc: Any active editing session from {{label}} will be disconnected, and future signed updates from it will be rejected.

encryption.title: End-to-end encrypted
encryption.body: File names and contents are encrypted on every device. Admins can manage accounts but cannot read user data.

overview.footnote: Activity feed, server uptime, and TLS status are not exposed by kutup's backend yet — they'll appear here when the endpoints land.

adminDialog.promoteDesc: They gain full access to this admin panel — managing users, settings, and storage. Takes effect on their next sign-in or token refresh.
adminDialog.demoteDesc: They lose access to this admin panel. Takes effect on their next sign-in or token refresh.

totpDialog.title: Disable 2FA for {{email}}?
totpDialog.desc: This makes the account password-only until the user re-enables 2FA from their Security page. Use this when a user is locked out of their authenticator.

breakGlass.tooltip: Break-glass admin — protected from demote, disable, and delete.

settings.registrationDesc: Control who can sign up for this Kutup instance.
settings.storageDesc: Read-only — change via your kutup.yml config / env vars.
settings.storageUsedUnknown: Capacity unknown — set STORAGE_TOTAL_BYTES or SEAWEEDFS_MASTER_URL to display.
settings.moreSoonNote: More admin controls — required 2FA, quota defaults, danger-zone actions — land as the backend grows.

createDialog.title: Create user
createDialog.desc: A temporary password is set now; the user replaces it on first sign-in.

deleteDialog.title: Delete {{email}}?
deleteDialog.desc: This permanently removes the user and all their data. This action cannot be undone.

publicShare.missingKey: Missing decryption key — the URL may be incomplete. The key must be in the #fragment.
publicShare.e2e: End-to-end encrypted — decrypted in your browser
publicShare.name: Name

expired.title: Link expired
expired.desc: This share link is no longer valid.

image.quotaExceeded: Storage quota exceeded — image not uploaded

shortcuts.title: Editor keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.desc: Available while editing. Save and search are page-wide; the rest fire only when the editor has focus.

empty.title: Nothing shared yet
empty.subtitle: Send a file to anyone — even without a Kutup account
empty.hint: Open a file and tap Share to start

trash.subtitle: Items are permanently deleted after 30 days

empty.title: Trash is empty
empty.subtitle: Deleted files appear here for 30 days

account.profile.note: Account details are managed by your Kutup server. Contact your admin to change them.
account.encryptionKeys.heroBody: Files are end-to-end encrypted with keys derived from your password. The 24-word recovery phrase is your only fallback if you forget it.
account.encryptionKeys.viewRecoverySub: Write down or print the 24 words
account.encryptionKeys.rotateSub: Generate a new phrase — invalidates the old one
account.encryptionKeys.footer: Kutup never sees your phrase — it stays on this device and the devices you sync it to.
account.security.note: Full setup (QR code, recovery codes, device revocation) is on the desktop Settings page for now.
account.notifications.emptySubtitle: Activity from shares, mentions, and updates will appear here.
account.security.devicesEmptyHint: Devices appear here when they start a collaborative edit.
account.security.totpOffDescription: Add an extra layer of security with an authenticator app

mobile.actionUnavailable: Tap a folder on desktop to create one — mobile flow coming soon.

totp.setupInstructions: Open your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, 1Password, Authy, etc.) and add an account by scanning the QR code below — or pasting the key manually. Then enter the 6-digit code it generates.
totp.lossNote: If you lose access to your authenticator app, use your 24-word recovery phrase to restore the account.

admin.title: Admin
admin.overview.e2eNote: File names and contents are encrypted on every device. Admins can manage accounts but cannot read user data.
admin.users.emptySubtitle: Try a different filter or search
admin.user.title: User
admin.user.editQuotaDesc: Set the storage quota in gigabytes. Existing files are preserved.
admin.user.makeAdminSub: Grant full access to the admin panel
admin.user.removeAdminSub: Revoke access to the admin panel
admin.user.promoteDesc: They gain full access to the admin panel. Takes effect on their next sign-in.
admin.user.demoteDesc: They lose access to the admin panel. Takes effect on their next sign-in.
admin.user.disableTotpSub: For users locked out of their authenticator
admin.user.totpAlreadyOff: 2FA is not enabled for this user
admin.user.totpDesc: The account becomes password-only until the user re-enables 2FA from their Security page.
admin.user.disableSub: User cannot sign in but data is preserved
admin.user.deleteSub: All encrypted blobs will be removed
admin.user.deleteDesc: All encrypted blobs will be removed. This cannot be undone.
admin.createUser.title: New user
admin.createUser.intro: A temporary password is set now; the user replaces it on first sign-in and generates encryption keys client-side.
admin.createUser.tempPasswordHint: Share this with the user out-of-band; they change it on first sign-in.
admin.settings.publicRegSub: Anyone can create an account from the sign-up page
admin.settings.moreSoonNote: More admin controls — required 2FA, quota defaults, storage backend, danger-zone actions — land as the backend grows. The desktop /admin page covers anything missing here.
